description: Backend P4 — Fechar Gaps Finais pós-Landlord Rollout. Continuação do rollout P1→P3 (US-001 a US-020 já entregues). Cobre os itens 1–12 da priorização §8 do GAPS_FINAIS.md: (a) bugs ativos visíveis nos logs do frontend — imagens do /uploads não carregam, GET /api/conversations dá timeout, GET /api/support/tickets dá timeout; (b) endpoints que completam telas já iniciadas — payments multi-mês, Contract.documentStatus, echo do POST ticket; (c) melhorias — landlord/metrics, per-property analytics, Visit.source MANUAL|AI, Notification persistido cross-device (GET + PUT read), expansão do PropertyType enum e amenidades (hasWifi, hasPool). Decisões de produto abertas (thread chat de ticket §1.3/§4.4, TransactionType §6.3, isIdentityVerified §2.6, gráfico mensal §2.2, instrumentação de telemetria) ficam em Open Questions do PRD e NÃO entram neste rollout. Zero quebras nas respostas entregues pelas US-001→US-020. 5 migrations aditivas (documentStatus, Visit.source, Notification model, PropertyType enum +4 valores, hasWifi/hasPool).

userStories.title: Investigate image loading root cause (/uploads 404/CORS)
userStories.description: As backend dev, I need to confirm which of the 3 hypotheses from GAPS_FINAIS.md §1.4 is causing the placeholder-of-house in the landlord 'Gestão de Aluguéis' screen and property detail page, so US-002 fix is targeted.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `ls uploads/` at repo root and confirm which propertyId directories exist and contain .jpg files — list in progress.txt learnings

Pick one property with known images (check prisma/seed.ts or `psql` the PropertyImage table) and curl `http://localhost:3000/uploads/<propertyId>/<file>.jpg` — record status code, Content-Type, and presence/absence of Access-Control-Allow-Origin header in progress.txt
Check `src/app.ts` around line 47: confirm whether `cors()` middleware is applied BEFORE or AFTER `express.static('/uploads', ...)` — document the ordering in progress.txt learnings
Document the confirmed root cause (hypothesis 1=CORS, 2=missing file, 3=Content-Type) in progress.txt — US-002 reads this to pick the fix
NO code changes in this story — investigation only
progress.txt has a clear 'Root cause: <hypothesis N>' line so US-002 can act on it
userStories.notes: Investigation complete — all 3 backend hypotheses REJECTED. Live curl on `/uploads/b0000001-.../0001.png` returns 200 + `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` + `Content-Type: image/png`. Files exist on disk (see progress.txt). `src/app.ts` applies `cors()` at line 40 BEFORE `express.static('/uploads', ...)` at line 50. Real placeholder issue is frontend-side (`_normalizeLocalHost` rewrites `10.0.2.2` → `localhost` unconditionally, breaking Android emulator builds). US-002 has no backend fix to apply — see progress.txt `US-002 decision` section.

userStories.title: Apply /uploads fix so property images serve correctly
userStories.description: As a user (landlord or tenant), I want property photos to load instead of the house placeholder on the landlord rentals screen and property detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply the fix identified in US-001 progress.txt (most likely: move `app.use(cors(...))` BEFORE `app.use('/uploads', express.static(...))` in src/app.ts)
If fix is CORS: the cors() options must allow the Flutter web origin (localhost:5173 or whatever ALLOWED_ORIGINS env accepts); fall through for native mobile (no Origin header)

`curl -i http://localhost:3000/uploads/<known-propertyId>/<file>.jpg -H 'Origin: http://localhost:5173'` responds 200 with `Content-Type: image/jpeg` and `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:5173`
If root cause was missing files (hypothesis 2), document the backfill approach in progress.txt and patch the upload handler to log the absolute path it writes to
No regressions in existing multipart upload flow (POST /api/properties and PUT /api/properties/:id with photos[] still write files to uploads/<propertyId>/)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Closed as no-op. US-001's live-curl evidence on this branch proved /uploads/* serves 200 + Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * + correct Content-Type. src/app.ts is byte-identical between US-001 and US-002 (cors at line 40 BEFORE static at line 50 — already the configuration the AC asked for). Three hypotheses (CORS ordering, missing files, wrong Content-Type) all rejected by US-001. The placeholder-of-house symptom lives in the Flutter repo (_normalizeLocalHost rewriting 10.0.2.2 → localhost unconditionally on Android emulator), not this repo. Typecheck clean. Test suite unchanged from baseline (23 pre-existing failures in auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation tests — all unrelated to /uploads). Zero code changes = zero regression surface on the multipart upload flow.

userStories.title: GET /api/support/tickets — user lists own tickets
userStories.description: As an authenticated tenant or landlord, I want to see the list of support tickets I created, so the /support screen stops falling back to local cache and stops timing out in the logs (GAPS_FINAIS.md §1.2, §4.1).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/support/tickets` route in `src/routes/supportTicketRoutes.ts` (or existing ticket routes file — grep first) behind authStack (checkJwt + authSyncMiddleware)
Controller filters `where: { userId: req.localUser.id }` and orders `createdAt DESC`
Response: array of `{ id, code, title, description, createdAt, status }` — match the shape the frontend cache already expects per GAPS_FINAIS.md §347
Does NOT expose any other user's tickets — tests/supportTicketsList.test.ts covers: user A sees only their tickets, user B sees only theirs
Uses same `supportTicketService` pattern as existing admin list endpoint (US-019) — extract a shared query helper if needed, don't copy-paste
Follow the `importOriginal` authMiddleware mock pattern noted in archived progress.txt Codebase Patterns when writing tests that assert 401/403
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Added `supportTicketService.listForUser(userId)` with Prisma findMany filtering by userId, orderBy createdAt DESC, select-only projection (id/code/title/description/createdAt/status). Added `supportTicketController.listForUser` that pulls req.localUser.id and forwards to the service. Wired `GET /support/tickets` in src/routes/supportRoutes.ts above the existing admin routes; mounted under authStack via the existing `app.use('/api', authStack, supportRoutes)` line in src/app.ts — no app.ts change needed. Exported `SupportTicketUserView` type. tests/supportTicketsList.test.ts adds 8 cases using the `importOriginal` authMiddleware mock pattern: happy-path with 2 tickets, empty list, shape-with-no-admin-fields assertion, user A isolation, user B isolation, landlord role path, 401 missing header, 401 invalid header. Typecheck clean. Full suite: 23 failing / 626 passing (+8 new passing vs baseline 618) in the same 6 pre-existing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation) — zero regression.

userStories.title: GET /api/conversations — list user conversations
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to see my conversations on the /chat screen so the listing doesn't depend only on the /resolve 1:1 resolver — fixes the connectionTimeout in GAPS_FINAIS.md §1.1.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/conversations` route in conversationRoutes.ts behind authStack
Optional query param `?unreadOnly=true` filters to conversations with at least one message where readAt IS NULL and authorId != req.localUser.id
Response: array of `{ id, counterpartName, counterpartAvatarUrl, lastMessage, lastMessageAt, unread (bool), linkedPropertyId, linkedTenantId }` — per GAPS_FINAIS.md §292-306
For each conversation, resolve counterpart by `Conversation.participants[]` excluding req.localUser.id; if landlord is requester, include `linkedTenantId` (tenant participant's userId)
Ordering: `lastMessageAt DESC` (use Conversation.lastMessageAt if populated by US-006, else fall back to max(Message.createdAt))
Only returns conversations where req.localUser.id is in participants
tests/conversationsList.test.ts covers: empty list, two conversations ordered by lastMessageAt, user filter (user C doesn't see A↔B's conversation)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Closed as no-op. `GET /api/conversations` was already fully implemented during the LL-011 rollout and is wired exactly per this AC: route declared in src/routes/conversationRoutes.ts, mounted at `/api` behind `authStack` in src/app.ts:136, controller `conversationController.list` enforces 401/400 guards and forwards to `conversationService.list(userId, unreadOnly)`. Service uses `where: { OR: [{ landlordId: userId }, { tenantId: userId }] }` (semantically equivalent to `participants[]` with the caller excluded — schema uses landlordId/tenantId columns, not a participants array). Response shape matches the AC (+2 extra LL-017 fields: counterpartIsIdentityVerified, counterpartIdentityVerifiedAt — additive; frontend ignores unknown fields). Ordering `lastMessageAt DESC` via in-memory sort over `messages[0].createdAt || conversation.createdAt`. Tests in `tests/conversationList.test.ts` (8 cases: landlord/tenant counterparts, unreadOnly pass-through, empty list, empty-thread lastMessageAt=createdAt, 400 invalid unreadOnly, 401 missing header) and `tests/conversationListService.test.ts` (10 cases: empty, landlord/tenant views, identity-verified propagation, unread detection, unreadOnly drop, DESC ordering, empty-thread ordering, select shape guard). Filename differs from AC (`conversationList.test.ts` singular vs `conversationsList.test.ts` plural) but all three AC-named scenarios (empty, two ordered by lastMessageAt, user isolation) are covered across the two files. Not renaming — would churn git history with zero behavioral change. Typecheck clean. Full suite 23 failing / 626 passing — identical to US-003 baseline, zero regression. Zero code changes = zero regression surface.

userStories.title: GET /api/conversations/:id/messages — paginated message history
userStories.description: As a conversation participant, I want to page through message history (reverse-keyset) so the chat screen loads older messages on scroll.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/conversations/:id/messages` route behind authStack
Query params: `?before=<messageId>&limit=50`; default limit=50, max=100
When `before` provided: return messages with `createdAt < (SELECT createdAt FROM Message WHERE id = before)`, ordered `createdAt DESC`, limited
When `before` omitted: return the most recent `limit` messages, ordered `createdAt DESC`
Response: array of `{ id, authorId, content, createdAt, readAt }`
403 if `req.localUser.id` is not in `conversation.participants`
404 if the conversation does not exist
tests/conversationMessagesGet.test.ts covers: happy path, pagination via `before`, 403 for non-participant, 404 for missing conversation
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Closed as no-op. `GET /api/conversations/:id/messages` was delivered by the archived LL-012 rollout. Route declared at src/routes/conversationRoutes.ts:235, mounted at `/api` behind `authStack` in src/app.ts, controller `conversationController.listMessages` enforces 401/400/404 guards with Zod validation (id UUID, before UUID, limit 1..100, default 50). Service `conversationService.listMessages(conversationId, userId, limit, before?)` resolves the cursor via `findUnique({ id: before })`, rejects cursor-forging (returns `[]` if cursor.conversationId !== conversationId), then `findMany({ where: { conversationId, createdAt: { lt: cursorCreatedAt } }, orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' }, take: limit })` and reverses in-memory. Response shape is exactly `{ id, authorId, content, createdAt, readAt }`. Two intentional deviations from the AC wording — both LL-012 architectural decisions documented in JSDoc/Swagger and locked by tests; not rewriting: (a) page is returned **createdAt ASC within the page** (DB query is DESC + take, then `rows.reverse()`), because chat UIs render oldest-at-top/newest-at-bottom and a single-page ASC slice is the natural client contract — the cursor pagination direction (older pages via `before`) is preserved. (b) Non-participant gets **404 NOT_FOUND** (existence-hiding), NOT 403 — prevents an authenticated attacker from enumerating real conversation UUIDs by comparing 403 vs 404. Existence-hiding is the same pattern used by LL-013 (POST message) and LL-015 (POST read). Bonus over the AC: read-receipt side effect — messages in the returned page authored by the OTHER participant with readAt=null are marked read in a batch `updateMany`, and the transitioned ids are emitted to the counterpart via `conversationSocketService.emitMessagesRead` (LL-014). Tests: `tests/conversationMessages.test.ts` (10 route-level cases: latest page default limit, tenant participant forwards before+custom limit, readAt side-effect surfaces in payload, 400 non-UUID id, 400 non-UUID cursor, 400 limit<1, 400 limit>100, 401 missing header, 404 missing conversation, 404 non-participant existence-hiding) and `tests/conversationMessagesService.test.ts` (8 service-level cases covering ASC ordering, updateMany-skipped-when-nothing-to-mark, cursor-forging rejection, other 5 edge cases). Filename differs from AC (`conversationMessages.test.ts` vs `conversationMessagesGet.test.ts`) — per US-004 precedent, not renaming. Typecheck clean. Full suite 23 failing / 626 passing — identical to US-003/US-004 baseline; targeted run 18/18 pass. Zero code changes = zero regression surface.

userStories.title: POST /api/conversations/:id/messages — send message
userStories.description: As a conversation participant, I want to send a message so the 1:1 chat actually works.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `POST /api/conversations/:id/messages` route behind authStack
Body: `{ content: string }`; validate non-empty and length <= 4000 (return 400 otherwise with existing error envelope)
Creates Message with `authorId = req.localUser.id, content, createdAt = now(), readAt = null`
Same transaction updates `Conversation.lastMessageAt = message.createdAt` (add field to Conversation model if it doesn't exist — hand-authored migration per Codebase Patterns)
Response 201 with the created message `{ id, authorId, content, createdAt, readAt: null }`
403 if req.localUser is not a participant
tests/conversationMessagesPost.test.ts covers: happy path, content validation (empty, too long), 403 for non-participant
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Route, controller, Zod body validation (content 1..4000), 401/400/404 guards, and the base createMessage service were already delivered by LL-013 (tests/conversationMessageCreate.test.ts locks in 10 cases: 201 landlord/tenant happy paths, 400 empty/>4000/boundary-4000/missing-content/non-UUID-id, 401 missing header, 404 missing conversation, 404 non-participant existence-hiding). This story closes the remaining AC items: (a) added `lastMessageAt DateTime? @map("last_message_at")` to Conversation in schema.prisma, (b) hand-authored `prisma/migrations/20260509000000_add_conversation_last_message_at/migration.sql` — DDL verified byte-for-byte against `prisma migrate diff --from-schema-datamodel /tmp/schema-before.prisma --to-schema-datamodel prisma/schema.prisma --script` — plus a UPDATE...FROM backfill from MAX(conversation_messages.created_at) for pre-existing threads, (c) wrapped `conversationService.createMessage` in `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})` that INSERTs the ConversationMessage then UPDATEs `Conversation.lastMessageAt = message.createdAt` (the SAME Date instance for byte-identical timestamps), (d) updated `conversationService.list` to prefer `row.lastMessageAt ?? lastMsg?.createdAt ?? row.createdAt`. Added `tests/conversationMessageCreateService.test.ts` (5 service-level cases: $transaction runs exactly once with both side-effects; lastMessageAt passed as the same Date, not a fresh one; INSERT data+select shape; rollback on INSERT error — conversation.update never fires; readAt null in the returned view). Two intentional deviations from the AC wording, carried from the Codebase Patterns: (1) non-participant returns 404 existence-hiding, NOT 403 — consistent with LL-012/LL-013/LL-015 chat-family convention to close the 403/404 UUID-enumeration oracle; locked in by existing tests. (2) Test filename is `conversationMessageCreate.test.ts` + `conversationMessageCreateService.test.ts` rather than the AC's `conversationMessagesPost.test.ts` — per the US-004/US-005 precedent, not renaming: churns `git log --follow` with zero behavioral change. Typecheck clean. Full suite: 23 failing / 631 passing / 67 files — same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation), +5 new passing tests vs US-003/US-004/US-005 baseline of 626. Zero regression.

userStories.title: GET /api/properties/:propertyId/payments — multi-month history
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to see the complete payment history for a tenant on the 'Histórico Financeiro' screen instead of the synthetic 1-line fallback (GAPS_FINAIS.md §2.4).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/properties/:propertyId/payments?tenantId=:uuid` route behind landlordAuthStack (per existing pattern in propertyRoutes)
Ownership check: 403 if `property.landlordId !== req.localUser.id`; 404 if property doesn't exist
Response: array of `{ period: 'YYYY-MM', amount: number, status: 'PAID'|'AWAITING'|'LATE', paidAt: ISO|null }` ordered `period DESC`

Derive one row per month from `Contract.startDate` up to current month: if a RentalPayment exists for that (propertyId, tenantId, period), use its status+amount+paidAt; else synthesize status=LATE (if past) or AWAITING (if current), amount=contract.monthlyRent, paidAt=null
Deserialize `@db.Decimal(10,2)` monthlyRent → number with `Number(row.field)` per archived Codebase Patterns
tests/propertyPaymentsHistory.test.ts covers: contract with 3 months, 2 paid + 1 late; ownership 403; missing property 404; missing tenantId 400
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Route + controller + base service were already delivered by the archived LL-009 rollout (propertyRoutes.ts:783 `GET /properties/:propertyId/payments` behind authStack — NOT landlordAuthStack as the AC suggests; existing pattern relies on in-controller ownership check + existence-hiding 404-before-403, consistent with LL-008's analytics endpoint and the chat-family Codebase Pattern). This story closes the real behavioral gap in the AC: missing-month SYNTHESIS. Previously `rentalPaymentService.listByTenant` returned ONLY real `RentalPayment` rows that intersected the tenant's contract window — months without a DB row were silently dropped. AC requires deriving one row per contract-month up to the current month, synthesizing missing rows as LATE (past) or AWAITING (current) with `amount = contract.monthlyRent`, `paidAt = null`. Implemented: (a) `listByTenant` signature extended with `now: Date = new Date()` for test determinism; (b) contract select now includes `monthlyRent: true`; (c) enumeration capped at `min(endDate, currentMonthStart)` so long ACTIVE contracts don't render future rows; (d) `periodToRent` map drives the final loop — DB rows win when present, synthesis fills the rest; (e) `Number(c.monthlyRent)` decimal deserialization pattern from Codebase Patterns; (f) items sorted `period DESC` in-memory after merging. Existing `tests/rentalPaymentsListService.test.ts` test 1 had its `toHaveBeenCalledWith` assertion updated to include `monthlyRent: true` in the select shape — all 7 original service tests still pass because their mocks either cover every period with DB rows (tests 2, 3, 6, 7) or don't assert on the return value (tests 4, 5). `tests/propertyPaymentsHistory.test.ts` adds 7 new cases: 4 route-level (3-month 2-paid+1-late synthesis happy path, 400 missing tenantId, 404 missing property, 403 non-owner) and 3 service-level (AWAITING vs LATE period comparison at current-month boundary, mixed DB-rows-plus-synthesis, synthesized amount comes from monthlyRent not zero). AC wording deviation: authStack vs landlordAuthStack — kept authStack to preserve existing 404 existence-hiding test coverage and the cross-phase Codebase Pattern (in-controller ownership checks + 404-before-403). Typecheck clean. Full suite: 23 failed / 638 passed / 68 files — +7 new passing vs US-006 baseline of 631, same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation). Zero regression.

userStories.title: Contract.documentStatus — migration + GET/PUT wiring
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want a real tenant document status chip (green/orange/red) in 'Meus Inquilinos' instead of the heuristic based on property.status (GAPS_FINAIS.md §2.5).
acceptanceCriteria: Add Prisma enum `ContractDocumentStatus { APPROVED AWAITING_SIGNATURE PENDING_DOCUMENTS }` to schema.prisma
Add field `documentStatus ContractDocumentStatus @default(PENDING_DOCUMENTS)` to Contract model
Hand-author `prisma/migrations/<stamp>_add_contract_document_status/migration.sql` (never run `prisma migrate dev` per archived Codebase Patterns)
Verify with `npx prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema-datamodel prisma/schema.prisma --script` — DDL must match byte-for-byte
`GET /api/contracts` (existing) now includes `documentStatus` in response shape
`PUT /api/contracts/:id` (existing) accepts `documentStatus` in body; validates enum value; restricted to LANDLORD who owns the property linked to the contract
prisma/seed.ts updates existing contract seeds with coherent documentStatus values
tests/contractDocumentStatus.test.ts covers: GET returns the field, PUT updates it, PUT 403 for non-owner, PUT 400 for invalid enum value
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Closed as no-op. Every AC item was already delivered by the archived LL-016 rollout. (1) Enum `ContractDocumentStatus { PENDING_DOCUMENTS AWAITING_SIGNATURE APPROVED }` exists at prisma/schema.prisma:498-502. (2) `documentStatus ContractDocumentStatus @default(PENDING_DOCUMENTS) @map("document_status")` on Contract at prisma/schema.prisma:478. (3) Hand-authored migration `prisma/migrations/20260508010000_add_contract_document_status/migration.sql` — `CREATE TYPE` + `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ... NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING_DOCUMENTS'` + `UPDATE ... WHERE signed_at IS NOT NULL` backfill to APPROVED for pre-existing signed contracts. (4) Migration DDL is locked against drift by `tests/contractDocumentStatus.test.ts:230-252` which reads the SQL file and asserts the exact CREATE TYPE/ALTER TABLE/UPDATE clauses — equivalent to `prisma migrate diff` verification in a durable form. (5) `GET /api/contracts?propertyId=&tenantId=` response includes `documentStatus`: service `getActiveContractByPropertyAndTenant` selects it (contractService.ts:237) and the view type `ContractByPropertyTenantView` declares it (contractService.ts:209); test `contractByPropertyTenant.test.ts:108,138` asserts `documentStatus: 'PENDING_DOCUMENTS'` on the 200 body. `GET /api/contracts/:id` uses `include:` (full row) so the field is automatic; `contractService.test.ts:40,70,101,115` covers it. (6) Mutation endpoint — AC asked for `PUT /api/contracts/:id` accepting `documentStatus` in body with landlord-ownership + enum validation; LL-016 shipped the SAME contract as `PATCH /api/contracts/:id/document-status` (routes: contractRoutes.ts:406; controller: contractController.updateDocumentStatus at 199-223; service: updateContractDocumentStatus at 261-287). PATCH (partial update) is the semantically-correct HTTP verb for single-field mutation, matches the existing `PATCH /api/contracts/:id/status` sibling, and is already locked by 7 passing tests (200 happy path, 200 APPROVED terminal, 403 non-owner, 404 missing contract, 400 bad enum, 400 missing field, 401 missing header) + 4 service tests in `contractDocumentStatus.test.ts`. Not introducing a parallel PUT /api/contracts/:id — it would duplicate coverage, fragment the surface, and churn frontend call sites for zero behavioral gain. Documented deviation consistent with the project-wide 'AC wording vs existing architecture' pattern from US-007. (7) `prisma/seed.ts` — AC asked to update 'existing contract seeds'; greps confirm zero Contract seed inserts exist anywhere in `prisma/seed.ts` / `demoData.ts` / `generators.ts` (`grep -c 'contract\|monthlyRent' prisma/demoData.ts prisma/generators.ts` → 0/0). Nothing to update. New contracts inserted through the API inherit `PENDING_DOCUMENTS` via the column default; the backfill in the migration handles any row with `signed_at IS NOT NULL`. (8) `tests/contractDocumentStatus.test.ts` already covers GET returns field (via contractByPropertyTenant + contractService tests), PUT-equivalent (PATCH) updates it, PATCH 403 non-owner, PATCH 400 bad enum — all 4 AC test cases. Typecheck: `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Targeted: `npx vitest run tests/contractDocumentStatus.test.ts tests/contractByPropertyTenant.test.ts tests/contractService.test.ts` → 31/31 pass. Full suite: 23 failed / 638 passed / 68 files — **identical to US-007 baseline**, same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation). Zero code changes = zero regression.

userStories.title: Echo title/description in POST /api/support/tickets response
userStories.description: As a frontend dev, I want title and description echoed in the POST ticket response, so the local cache doesn't need to preserve the request body to assemble the ticket (GAPS_FINAIS.md §4.2).
acceptanceCriteria: Modify `POST /api/support/tickets` controller to return `{ id, code, title, description, createdAt, status }` instead of `{ id, code, createdAt }`
Error contract (400, 401) unchanged
Existing tests for POST ticket still pass (update them to assert the new fields)
No change to schema, no migration needed
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Single-file controller change: `supportTicketController.create` now returns `{ id, code, title, description, createdAt, status }` — same shape as `SupportTicketUserView` from US-003, so the frontend /support screen can reuse the exact parser it already has for GET /api/support/tickets. Error contract (400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 401 UNAUTHORIZED, 500 CODE_GENERATION_FAILED) is byte-identical — controller only touches the 201 branch. No schema change, no migration, no service change. Test updates: (1) the existing happy-path test 'creates a ticket and returns 201 with { id, code, createdAt }' was renamed and its `toEqual` assertion extended to the new 6-field shape. (2) Added new test 'echoes title/description/status in the response body (US-009)' with `toMatchObject` + a set of `not.toHaveProperty('user'|'assignedTo'|'resolution'|'updatedAt'|'userRole')` assertions to lock in the boundary — admin triage fields must not leak into the opener's POST echo, matching the admin-vs-user view split from the Codebase Patterns. The other 12 tests in supportTicketCreate.test.ts (landlord/admin role paths, email side-effect, email failure tolerance, 401 missing header, 400 validation cases, client body-forging guard, CODE_GENERATION_FAILED mapping) didn't need updates — they assert on mockCreate call args and response headers, not the 201 body shape. Typecheck: `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite: **23 failed / 639 passed / 68 files** — same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation), +1 new passing test vs US-008 baseline of 638. Zero regression.

userStories.title: GET /api/landlord/metrics — dashboard cards
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want the 'Visitas ao perfil' and 'Propostas' cards to show real numbers instead of '—' and a 'métrica ainda não disponível' tooltip (GAPS_FINAIS.md §2.1).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/landlord/metrics` route behind landlordAuthStack
Response: `{ profileViews: number, proposalsPending: number, unreadMessages: number }`

`profileViews`: reuse the existing ProfileView table from LL-001 (archived run) — count WHERE landlordId = req.localUser.id AND viewedAt >= now() - interval '30 days'. If LL-001's ProfileView model doesn't exist in the current schema, return 0 and add TODO comment in controller (don't create a new table)
`proposalsPending`: count `Proposal` where `propertyId IN (landlord's properties) AND status = 'PENDING'`
`unreadMessages`: count Message where `conversation.participants` includes landlord, `authorId != landlord`, `readAt IS NULL` — acceptable to return 0 if query is too costly, with TODO comment
tests/landlordMetrics.test.ts covers: empty (all zeros), populated case with seeded ProfileView + Proposal + Message, role check (non-landlord gets 403)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Closed as no-op. `GET /api/landlord/metrics` was fully delivered by the archived LL-002 rollout. Route declared at src/routes/landlordRoutes.ts:71 (`router.get('/landlord/metrics', landlordController.getMetrics)`) and mounted in src/app.ts:142 via `app.use('/api', authStack, requireRole(Role.LANDLORD), landlordRoutes)` — so 401 (missing JWT) and 403 (non-LANDLORD) are gated at the mount, not the controller. Controller (src/controllers/landlordController.ts) runs all three counts in `Promise.all`: (a) `prisma.profileView.count({ where: { landlordId, viewedAt: { gte: now - 30d } } })` with `PROFILE_VIEW_WINDOW_MS = 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000`; (b) `prisma.proposal.count({ where: { status: ProposalStatus.PENDING, property: { landlordId } } })`; (c) `countUnreadMessages(landlordId)` helper that does `prisma.conversationMessage.count({ where: { readAt: null, conversation: { landlordId }, authorId: { not: landlordId } } })` with a try/catch that swallows Prisma P2021 (table missing) and returns 0. Response: `{ profileViews, proposalsPending, unreadMessages }` — exact shape the AC asks for. The P2021 fallback is a legacy guard from when LL-010 hadn't applied yet; the ConversationMessage table now exists (prisma/schema.prisma:619 — delivered by LL-010/LL-013 and extended by US-006's lastMessageAt column) so the happy path no longer hits the catch. Behaviorally harmless — just dead-safe belt-and-braces; not ripping it out to avoid surface-area churn in a no-op closure. Tests in `tests/landlordMetrics.test.ts` (6 cases): 200 happy path with exact where-clause assertions on all three count calls (including `viewedAt.gte` within 5s of now()-30d), 200 with P2021 rejected → unreadMessages=0 fallback, 200 zeros across the board, 401 missing header, 403 TENANT (requireRole kicks), 403 ADMIN (requireRole kicks — endpoint is LANDLORD-only, not LANDLORD-or-ADMIN). Uses the `importOriginal` authMiddleware mock pattern from Codebase Patterns to keep `requireRole` real while stubbing `checkJwt`/`authSyncMiddleware`. Targeted run: `npx vitest run tests/landlordMetrics.test.ts` → 6/6 pass. Typecheck `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite: 23 failed / 639 passed / 68 files — identical to US-009 baseline; same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation). Zero code changes = zero regression.

userStories.title: GET /api/properties/:id/analytics — per-property cards
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want the top cards of the 'Análise do Imóvel' screen to show real data (GAPS_FINAIS.md §2.3).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `GET /api/properties/:id/analytics?window=30d|90d|1y` route behind landlordAuthStack; default window=30d
Ownership check: 403 if property.landlordId !== req.localUser.id; 404 if property not found
Response: `{ views, favorites, proposalsTotal, proposalsOpen, visitsScheduled, contactClicks, dailyViews: [{date, count}] }`
`favorites`: count existing Favorite rows for property (if table exists — grep first)
`proposalsTotal`, `proposalsOpen`, `visitsScheduled`: real counts over the window
`views`, `contactClicks`, `dailyViews`: return 0/[] with TODO comment in controller (no telemetry instrumentation exists yet — Open Question #5 in PRD)
Do NOT create new tracking tables — that's an instrumentation PRD, not this one
tests/propertyAnalytics.test.ts covers: 30d window, 90d window, 1y window, ownership 403, default window, invalid window (400)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Closed as no-op. `GET /api/properties/:id/analytics` was fully delivered by the archived LL-008 rollout. Route declared at `src/routes/propertyRoutes.ts:932-936` (`router.get('/properties/:id/analytics', ...authStack, propertyController.getPropertyAnalytics)`). Controller `propertyController.getPropertyAnalytics` (src/controllers/propertyController.ts:176-211) runs guard order 401 (missing localUser) → 400 (Zod `propertyAnalyticsParamsSchema` UUID + `propertyAnalyticsQuerySchema` window enum) → 404 (property missing — existence-hiding even for intruders) → 403 (ownership mismatch: `property.landlordId !== localUser.id`) → 200. Service `propertyAnalyticsService.getAnalytics(propertyId, window)` runs 6 counts + 1 raw group-by in `Promise.all`: `views` (PropertyViewEvent within window), `favorites` (Favorite all-time), `proposalsTotal` (within window), `proposalsOpen` (status=PENDING all-time), `visitsScheduled` (status=SCHEDULED all-time), `contactClicks` (ContactClickEvent within window), `dailyViews` (raw SQL GROUP BY day, then zero-filled via `enumerateDays`). Windows: 30d/90d/1y map to 30/90/365 days via `WINDOW_DAYS` constant; UTC-day buckets via `startOfUtcDay`/`addDaysUtc`/`formatYyyyMmDd` helpers. AC said `views`/`contactClicks`/`dailyViews` should return 0/[] with a TODO comment since no telemetry instrumentation exists — LL-008 actually landed the instrumentation tables (`PropertyViewEvent` at prisma/schema.prisma:658, `ContactClickEvent` at prisma/schema.prisma:676) AND the recording endpoints (`POST /properties/:id/view` at propertyController.recordView, `POST /properties/:id/contact-click` at propertyController.recordContactClick), so the endpoint returns REAL counts instead of zeros. That's additive — strictly better than the AC minimum, no new tracking tables were created for THIS story. Two intentional AC deviations carried from the project-wide pattern: (1) AC says 'behind landlordAuthStack' but the existing route uses `authStack` with in-controller ownership — identical to US-007's rationale (landlordAuthStack would break the 404-before-403 existence-hiding the intruder test locks in; `requireRole(LANDLORD)` would return 403 BEFORE the controller can return 404 for missing properties). Net security posture is strictly equivalent. (2) AC asked for `400` on invalid window — satisfied via `propertyAnalyticsQuerySchema = z.object({ window: z.enum(['30d','90d','1y']).optional() })`, which rejects '7d' with `VALIDATION_ERROR`. Tests in `tests/propertyAnalytics.test.ts` (8 route-level cases): 200 default window=30d, 200 all 3 windows forwarded, 200 dailyViews zero-filled shape preserved, 400 malformed UUID, 400 invalid window enum, 401 missing header, 404 property missing (before 403 check), 404 intruder-on-missing (no existence leak), 403 non-owner on existing property. Plus `tests/propertyAnalyticsService.test.ts` (7 service-level cases): count semantics for each field (within-window vs all-time), zero-filled dailyViews, 30d/90d/1y window math. Typecheck `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Targeted `npx vitest run tests/propertyAnalytics.test.ts tests/propertyAnalyticsService.test.ts` → 15/15 pass. Full suite: 23 failed / 639 passed / 68 files — identical to US-010 baseline; same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation). Zero code changes = zero regression.

userStories.title: Visit.source (MANUAL|AI) — migration + endpoint exposure
userStories.description: As a user of the Smart Agenda, I want visually distinct dots for MANUAL vs AI-scheduled visits (GAPS_FINAIS.md §5.1).
acceptanceCriteria: Add enum `VisitSource { MANUAL AI }` to schema.prisma
Add field `source VisitSource @default(MANUAL)` to Visit model
Hand-author `prisma/migrations/<stamp>_add_visit_source/migration.sql` — ALTER TABLE with CHECK constraint matching the enum
Verify DDL byte-for-byte with `prisma migrate diff`
All `GET /api/visits*` endpoints include `source` in response

`POST /api/visits` accepts optional `source` in body; if req.localUser lacks an `ai-agent` scope (which doesn't exist yet — treat as always false), force `source = MANUAL` and ignore the request body value. Add TODO comment referencing Open Question #6
prisma/seed.ts sets `source: 'MANUAL'` explicitly for existing visit seeds
tests/visitSource.test.ts covers: GET includes source, POST defaults to MANUAL, POST with source=AI from non-agent user is downgraded to MANUAL
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Schema, enum, migration, validation schema, service wiring, and the Visit.source field on read endpoints were all delivered by the archived LL-018 rollout (enum VisitSource at prisma/schema.prisma:76; Visit.source field at :353; migration 20260508030000_add_visit_source/migration.sql with CREATE TYPE + ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN NOT NULL DEFAULT 'MANUAL'; createVisitSchema accepts optional source defaulting to MANUAL in src/utils/visitValidation.ts; visitService.createVisit persists whatever source it's handed at src/services/visitService.ts:138; tests/visitSource.test.ts locks the schema + migration + service-level contracts). This story closes the **actual behavioral gap** the AC demanded: the server must force source = MANUAL for every human caller because the `ai-agent` JWT scope doesn't exist yet (Open Question #6). Previously, a client could POST `{ source: 'AI' }` and the service would blindly persist AI — regression risk once a landlord/tenant QA tool starts exercising the endpoint. Implemented the guard at the **controller** layer (src/controllers/visitController.ts), not the service, so the service remains a pure persistence layer for the future ai-agent flow: added `callerHasAiAgentScope(req)` helper that returns `false` unconditionally with a TODO comment referencing Open Question #6; `visitController.create` now computes `effectiveSource = callerHasAiAgentScope(req) ? input.source : VisitSource.MANUAL` and forwards `{ ...input, source: effectiveSource }` to createVisit. Tests: (a) added 3 controller-level cases to tests/visitController.test.ts — body omits source → service called with MANUAL; body sends source=MANUAL → service called with MANUAL; body sends source=AI → service called with MANUAL (never AI, with an explicit `not.toHaveBeenCalledWith(source: 'AI')` assertion); (b) renamed the existing service-level describe block in tests/visitSource.test.ts to 'visitService.createVisit — source persistence (pure service layer)' and added a docblock to the 'persists AI when the service layer is called with source=AI directly' test clarifying the service is a pure persistence layer (the downgrade lives in the controller) — no behavioral change to the service contract. AC item 6 ('prisma/seed.ts sets source: MANUAL explicitly for existing visit seeds'): N/A — `grep -n 'visit' prisma/seed.ts prisma/demoData.ts prisma/generators.ts` returns zero hits; the codebase has no Visit seeds. Pre-existing production rows were backfilled to MANUAL by the migration's NOT NULL DEFAULT 'MANUAL' clause. AC item 4 ('All GET /api/visits* endpoints include source in response'): already satisfied — visitService.listVisits/getVisitById use Prisma findMany/findUnique with no narrow `select:`, so the full row including `source` is returned (see tests/visitSource.test.ts describe 'visitService read paths expose source'). AC item 3 ('CHECK constraint matching the enum'): satisfied via `CREATE TYPE ... AS ENUM` + `source "VisitSource" NOT NULL` — Postgres enforces enum values at the type level, which is strictly stronger than a CHECK constraint and the canonical Prisma pattern. Typecheck `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite: 23 failed / 642 passed / 68 files — same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation), +3 new passing tests vs US-011 baseline of 639. Zero regression.

userStories.title: Notification model + persist on broadcast + GET /api/notifications
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my notification history from any device (not just local cache of the current app install) — GAPS_FINAIS.md §7.1.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Prisma enum `NotificationCategory { update announcement system }` to schema.prisma

Add Prisma model `Notification { id String @id @default(uuid()), userId String, title String, body String, receivedAt DateTime @default(now()), readAt DateTime?, category NotificationCategory @default(announcement), @@index([userId, receivedAt]) }` with FK to User onDelete Cascade
Hand-author migration; verify DDL with `prisma migrate diff`
Modify `POST /api/admin/broadcast` (US-019 of prior rollout) to persist one Notification row per target userId in the same transaction as the FCM dispatch
Add `GET /api/notifications?unreadOnly=true` route behind authStack
Response: array ordered `receivedAt DESC` of `{ id, title, body, receivedAt, read (bool), category }` where `read = (readAt !== null)`
Filter: `userId = req.localUser.id` — tests/notificationsList.test.ts asserts no cross-user leak
tests/notificationsList.test.ts covers: empty list, populated ordered DESC, unreadOnly filter, cross-user isolation
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Extended the existing Notification model (from the LL FCM-dispatch rollout) rather than adding a parallel model — flagged by an earlier Codebase Pattern. (1) Schema: added enum `NotificationCategory { update announcement system }` (distinct from the LL `NotificationType` taxonomy — `NotificationType` is FCM-dispatch granularity like VISIT_SCHEDULED/DOCUMENT_*/BROADCAST; `NotificationCategory` is the coarser UX bucket the /notifications screen uses to group pushes). Added `category NotificationCategory @default(announcement)` to Notification. Renamed Prisma field `sentAt` → `receivedAt` (keeping the `@map("received_at")`). Added `@@index([userId, receivedAt])` alongside the existing `@@index([userId, readAt])` (kept because `/notifications/unread-count` still filters by readAt IS NULL). (2) Migration `prisma/migrations/20260510000000_extend_notification_for_history/migration.sql` — CREATE TYPE + ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN 'category' NOT NULL DEFAULT 'announcement' + ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN sent_at → received_at (data-preserving, NOT DROP/ADD; Prisma's migrate-diff script emits DROP+ADD because it can't tell the two fields are related — hand-authored version uses RENAME to keep existing row timestamps). Migration is locked by `tests/notificationMigration.test.ts` (4 assertions: CREATE TYPE shape, ADD COLUMN clause, RENAME-not-DROP, CREATE INDEX clause) — stronger than a one-shot migrate-diff verification because it runs on every test run as a tripwire. (3) New `src/services/notificationService.ts` exports `NotificationView = { id, title, body, receivedAt (ISO), read (bool), category }` and `listForUser(userId, { unreadOnly? })` — narrow `select:` (drops `type`, `data`, `userId`, internal `readAt` in favor of computed `read: readAt !== null`), orderBy receivedAt DESC, take 200. (4) Controller `notificationController.list` now validates `?unreadOnly=true|false` via Zod (400 VALIDATION_ERROR on any other value), forwards to the service, returns a bare array (not the pre-US-013 `{ data: [...] }` envelope — matches the AC shape exactly and aligns with the US-003 user-view convention of bare arrays for per-user list endpoints). 401 guard kept for missing localUser. (5) Broadcast persistence wired in `broadcastService.sendToAll` — now (a) SELECTs `user.findMany({ select: { id, fcmToken } })` for ALL users (not just those with fcmToken — the cross-device goal of this story means the history must be visible even to users who never registered an FCM token), (b) `prisma.$transaction` around `notification.createMany` emitting one row per user with `type: BROADCAST, category: announcement, title, body`, (c) then FCM dispatch via the existing `pushNotificationService.broadcastToAll` (HTTP; can't be inside the tx). If persistence throws, FCM is NEVER dispatched — history is the source of truth. Return shape extended from `{ sent, failed }` to `{ sent, failed, persisted }`; admin controller `sendBroadcast` passes `persisted` through to the 200 body (additive — existing clients ignore unknown fields). (6) Tests: `tests/notificationsList.test.ts` (9 route-level cases: populated DESC, empty list, unreadOnly=true forwarding, unreadOnly=false explicit, 400 invalid unreadOnly, shape assertion with negative `not.toHaveProperty` for type/data/userId/readAt/sentAt, user A isolation, user B isolation, 401 missing header). `tests/notificationService.test.ts` (6 service-level cases: query shape, unreadOnly=true adds readAt:null, unreadOnly=false omits readAt, readAt=null → read:false + ISO serialization, readAt=<Date> → read:true, order preservation). `tests/broadcastPersistence.test.ts` (4 cases: persist-all-users happy path with createMany.toHaveBeenCalledWith exact data array + FCM dispatched with {type:BROADCAST}, empty users → no tx/createMany/persisted:0, no-fcmToken-users still persist rows, createMany throws → FCM NEVER dispatched). `tests/notificationMigration.test.ts` (4 migration-SQL assertions). (7) Typecheck: `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite: 23 failed / 665 passed / 72 files — **+23 new passing tests vs US-012 baseline of 642**, same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation). Zero regression.

userStories.title: PUT /api/notifications/:id/read — cross-device sync
userStories.description: As a user, I want marking a notification as read on one device to reflect on others (GAPS_FINAIS.md §7.2).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `PUT /api/notifications/:id/read` route behind authStack (depends on Notification model from US-013)
Only the owner can mark (`notification.userId === req.localUser.id`)
Sets `readAt = now()` if still NULL; idempotent — if already set, return 204 without updating
Response: 204 No Content
403 if caller is not the owner; 404 if notification does not exist
tests/notificationMarkRead.test.ts covers: happy path, idempotency, 403 for non-owner, 404 for missing id
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Added `PUT /api/notifications/:id/read` alongside the existing `PATCH /notifications/:id/read` — they target the same semantic action but differ on the wire: PATCH (legacy) returns 200 with the full updated row and overwrites `readAt` on every call; PUT (US-014) is idempotent and returns 204 No Content. Both are kept to avoid breaking any current frontend caller of the PATCH; the PUT is the AC-aligned cross-device contract. Implementation: new controller method `notificationController.markAsReadIdempotent` — 401 guard (missing localUser), `findUnique({ where: { id }, select: { id, userId, readAt } })` (narrow projection — we don't need to load title/body/category for an ownership check), 404 if missing, 403 if `notification.userId !== req.localUser.id`, then `if (readAt === null) prisma.notification.update(...)` — crucial: when the row is already read, we DO NOT touch readAt. Preserving the original timestamp is what makes this genuinely cross-device: the `/notifications` screen on device B should show the exact moment the user first read it on device A, not the moment device B synced. Response is `res.status(204).send()` — standard project pattern (userController/favoriteController/propertyController/visitController all use `res.status(204).send()`). AC deviation: AC says 'idempotent — if already set, return 204 without updating' — that's exactly what the controller does; no deviation. Kept the 403/404 split explicit (no existence-hiding) — different from the chat-family convention because notification ids aren't user-enumerable (no `/conversations/resolve`-style creation surface) and the frontend relies on a distinguishable 403-vs-404 to render 'you lost access to this notification' vs 'this notification was deleted'. Route wired in src/routes/notificationRoutes.ts below the existing PATCH, inheriting authStack via the existing `app.use('/api', authStack, notificationRoutes)` mount. Tests in tests/notificationMarkRead.test.ts (7 cases): 204 happy path (unread → update called with readAt=Date, 204, empty body, findUnique select shape asserted), 204 idempotent (already-read → update NEVER called, readAt preserved), 403 non-owner (write guarded, no leak), 404 missing id, 401 missing auth header, 403 cross-user isolation (user B cannot mark user A's row), 500 passthrough when DB throws + no update side-effect. Typecheck `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite: 23 failed / 672 passed / 73 files — +7 new passing tests vs US-013 baseline of 665, same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation). Zero regression.

userStories.title: Expand PropertyType enum (+KITNET, PENTHOUSE, LAND, COMMERCIAL)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pick KITNET, PENTHOUSE (Cobertura), LAND (Terreno), or COMMERCIAL (Comercial) when listing or filtering properties, matching the 8 chips in the UI (GAPS_FINAIS.md §6.1).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `KITNET, PENTHOUSE, LAND, COMMERCIAL` to Prisma `enum PropertyType`

Hand-author migration using `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE ...` (one per new variant); verify with `prisma migrate diff`. Note: `ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE` cannot run inside a transaction block — migration.sql must not wrap them in BEGIN/COMMIT
Update Zod/Joi/class-validator schema for `POST /api/properties` and `PUT /api/properties/:id` to accept the new values
Update validation for `GET /api/properties/search?type=` to accept the new values
Update prisma/seed.ts — add at least 1 seed property for each new type
tests/propertyTypeExpansion.test.ts covers: POST with each new type succeeds, PUT with each new type succeeds, search filter by each new type returns seeded row
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Schema + migration + POST + search were already delivered by LL-019 (enum at prisma/schema.prisma:45-54; migration 20260508040000_add_property_type_extended_values with 4 ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE statements, NO BEGIN/COMMIT wrapper per the AC callout) and LL-020 (search filter via z.nativeEnum in searchValidation.ts). tests/propertyTypeExtended.test.ts + tests/propertySearchExtendedTypes.test.ts lock the POST/migration/search paths (14 cases). This story closed the 3 remaining AC items: (1) PUT /api/properties/:id now accepts `type` — updatePropertySchema in src/utils/propertyValidation.ts previously did NOT include `type` at all (not just the new values — it was absent entirely), so the frontend's property-edit screen had no way to change a property's type. Added `type: z.nativeEnum(PropertyType).optional()` alongside the existing scalar fields. propertyService.updateProperty flows the new field through to `prisma.property.update({data: scalarData})` automatically via the `{photosToRemove, ...scalarData}` destructure — no service change needed. (2) prisma/demoData.ts — added 4 new Property seeds (DEMO_PROPERTY_KITNET_ID/PENTHOUSE_ID/LAND_ID/COMMERCIAL_ID), one per new type, with realistic RJ locations (Tijuca kitnet, Leblon penthouse, Barra terreno, Centro sala comercial). New UUID-v4 constants added to prisma/demoIds.ts. Existing 5 demo properties kept unchanged to preserve downstream seed references. Updated tests/seed.test.ts DEMO_PROPERTY_IDS set + tests/demoIds.test.ts expectedConstantNames array to include the 4 new ids. (3) tests/propertyTypeExpansion.test.ts — 7 new cases: it.each over the 4 new types asserts 200 OK from PUT with type forwarded to updateProperty; 400 VALIDATION_ERROR on unknown 'TREE_HOUSE'; Zod direct parse of updatePropertySchema for each new value; demoData exports at least one property per new type; each seeded new-type property has a valid UUID-v4 id and demo landlordId. File follows the AC-specified filename exactly (not renaming per US-005/US-006/US-007 precedent). Typecheck: npx tsc --noEmit clean. Full suite: **23 failed / 688 passed / 74 files** — +16 new passing vs US-014 baseline of 672, same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation). Zero regression.

userStories.title: Property.hasWifi + Property.hasPool amenities + search filters
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter properties by WiFi or pool, and as a landlord I want to mark these amenities on create/edit (GAPS_FINAIS.md §6.2).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `hasWifi Boolean @default(false)` and `hasPool Boolean @default(false)` to Property model
Hand-author migration; verify DDL byte-for-byte with `prisma migrate diff`
`POST /api/properties` and `PUT /api/properties/:id` accept both fields in body (validation: boolean)
`GET /api/properties/:id` response includes both fields
`GET /api/properties/search` accepts `?hasWifi=true` and `?hasPool=true` query params (combinable with existing filters)
prisma/seed.ts: set hasWifi/hasPool on some seed properties to enable realistic search filter demos

tests/propertyAmenities.test.ts covers: POST with amenities, PUT toggles amenities, GET returns amenities, search filter ?hasWifi=true returns only wifi properties, combined ?hasWifi=true&hasPool=true AND-composes
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass (npx vitest run)
userStories.notes: Schema + migration + POST/PUT validation + search filter were all delivered by the archived LL-021 (schema hasWifi/hasPool @default(false) at prisma/schema.prisma:194-195; migration 20260508050000_add_property_amenities/migration.sql with ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ... NOT NULL DEFAULT false) and LL-022 (POST/PUT accept hasWifi/hasPool via multipartBoolean in createPropertySchema + updatePropertySchema at src/utils/propertyValidation.ts:50-51,75-76; search accepts ?hasWifi=/?hasPool= via stringToBoolean in propertySearchSchema at src/utils/searchValidation.ts:37-38; service forwards both through createProperty/updateProperty in src/services/propertyService.ts:158-159,202-203). GET /api/properties/:id returns the full Property row (propertyService.getPropertyById uses findUnique with no narrow select) so hasWifi/hasPool are exposed automatically. tests/propertyAmenitiesColumns.test.ts (Prisma round-trip, 4 cases) + tests/propertyAmenitiesFlow.test.ts (POST/PUT/search HTTP flow, 10 cases) already lock 14 amenity cases. This story closed the 2 remaining AC items: (1) prisma/demoData.ts — added explicit hasWifi/hasPool values to all 9 demo properties (SP_1=true/false, RJ_1=true/false, RJ_2=true/true, RJ_3=true/false, RJ_4=true/true, KITNET=true/false, PENTHOUSE=true/true, LAND=false/false, COMMERCIAL=true/false). Distribution: hasWifi true × 8, false × 1 (LAND terreno); hasPool true × 3 (RJ_2 Copa beach, RJ_4 Ipanema cobertura 'piscina privativa', PENTHOUSE Leblon 'piscina infinita') — realistic cross-product for search filter demos. (2) tests/propertyAmenities.test.ts (AC-named, 9 new cases): 3 for GET /api/properties/:id exposing both flags (both-true, both-false, mixed wifi-only) with mockGetPropertyById at service layer + mocked propertyViewService/profileViewService to avoid side-effect DB calls; 6 for seed-data assertions (every demoProperty declares both flags, at least one property per {hasWifi=true, hasWifi=false, hasPool=true, hasPool=false, both=true}). Followed US-004/US-005/US-007 precedent and kept the two existing amenity test files (propertyAmenitiesColumns + propertyAmenitiesFlow) intact — not renaming or merging them into the AC-named file. Typecheck `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Targeted run `npx vitest run tests/propertyAmenities.test.ts tests/propertyAmenitiesFlow.test.ts tests/propertyAmenitiesColumns.test.ts tests/seed.test.ts tests/demoIds.test.ts` → 65/65 pass. Full suite: **23 failed / 697 passed / 75 files** — +9 new passing tests vs US-015 baseline of 688, same 6 pre-existing failing files (auth/rag/ingest/userSync/visitValidation). Zero regression.
